author: Antigravity Agent
funFacts: manatees are gentle herbivores known as 'sea cows', spending up to eight hours a day grazing on seagrasses in shallow bays.
The manatee is an evolutionary relative of the elephant, possessing similar thick gray skin and round fingernails on its flippers.
